thread.title: 21 women win in 288-seat Maharashtra assembly elections; Only one from opposition
thread.title_full: 21 women win in 288-seat Maharashtra assembly elections; Only one from opposition

author: https://www.cnbctv18.com
title: 21 women win in 288-seat Maharashtra assembly elections; Only one from opposition
text:
1 Min Read
A total of 21 women women in the Maharashtra 2024 elections to the 288-seat state assembly. Of them, just one candidate was from the opposition.
Of the 21 women, the BJP accounted for the highest number of candidates at 14, including the 10 women who were re-elected — Shweta Mahale (Chikli constituency), Meghana Bordikar (Jintur), Devyani Farande (Nashik Central), Seema Hiray (Nashik West), Manda Mhatre (Belapur), Manisha Chaudhari (Dahisar), Vidya Thakur (Goregaon), Madhuri Misal (Parvati), Monica Rajale (Shevgaon) and Namita Mundada (Kaij).
Manjula Gavit (Sakri) and Sanjana Jadhav (Kannad) were elected on the ruling Shiv Sena tickets, while Sulbha Khodke (Amravati), Saroj Ahire (Deolali), Sana Malik (Anushaktinagar) and Aditi Tatkare (Shrivardhan) won on the ruling NCP tickets.
Congress' Jyoti Gaikwad (Dhavari) is the lone woman from the opposition's side.
With inputs from PTI
Of the 21 women, the BJP accounted for the highest number of candidates at 14, including the 10 women who were re-elected — Shweta Mahale (Chikli constituency), Meghana Bordikar (Jintur), Devyani Farande (Nashik Central), Seema Hiray (Nashik West), Manda Mhatre (Belapur), Manisha Chaudhari (Dahisar), Vidya Thakur (Goregaon), Madhuri Misal (Parvati), Monica Rajale (Shevgaon) and Namita Mundada (Kaij).
The four new BJP winners were Shreejaya Chavan (Bhokar), Sulabha Gaikwad (Kalyan East), Sneha Pandit (Vasai) and Anuradha Chavan (Fulambari), according to the the poll results announced by the Election Commission of India (ECI) on Saturday, November 23.
Manjula Gavit (Sakri) and Sanjana Jadhav (Kannad) were elected on the ruling Shiv Sena tickets, while Sulbha Khodke (Amravati), Saroj Ahire (Deolali), Sana Malik (Anushaktinagar) and Aditi Tatkare (Shrivardhan) won on the ruling NCP tickets.
Congress' Jyoti Gaikwad (Dhavari) is the lone woman from the opposition's side.
With inputs from PTI
